title: Intensely Comforting Uruguayan Cocoa Bowl
description: A rich and comforting Uruguayan dish featuring cocoa powder, served in a traditional earthenware bowl, perfect for a cold evening. This egg-free friendly recipe is a cultural delight.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, whisk together the cocoa powder, sugar, and cornstarch.
Gradually add in the milk, whisking continuously to avoid lumps.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and comes to a boil.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the vanilla extract.
Pour the cocoa mixture into a preheated earthenware bowl or individual bowls.
Top each bowl with a spoonful of chewy dulce de leche and a sprinkle of crushed nuts, if using.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional nuts if desired.
Let the bowls cool slightly before serving, as the earthenware will retain heat.
Traditional Uruguayan stone bowls, or dolsot, can be used if available.
